review_summary: Transcript is sufficient for reinforcement questions on HSDP structure, topology-aware placement, and activation checkpointing tradeoffs. No separate clip is needed.

question_bank.question: What is Hybrid Sharded Data Parallelism (HSDP)?
choices: Using only pipeline parallelism across all GPUs
Using FSDP within groups of GPUs and ordinary data parallelism across groups
Alternating between tensor parallelism and context parallelism every step
Running one copy of the model per token position
question_bank.explanation: HSDP uses two axes: sharding within a local group for memory savings and replication across groups for broader data-parallel scaling.

question_bank.question: Why should the FSDP part of HSDP usually be placed on the faster local interconnects?
choices: Because FSDP communicates more heavily, roughly several model-weight transfers per forward/backward pass
Because data parallelism is impossible within a server
Because checkpointing only works on slow links
question_bank.explanation: The heavier communication pattern should stay on the faster links, while lighter outer data-parallel communication can tolerate slower links better.

transcript_quotes: So if we think about fully sharded parallelism,

question_bank.question: What tradeoff does activation checkpointing make?
choices: Less memory by storing fewer activations, at the cost of extra recomputation during backward
Less communication by duplicating every activation on every GPU
More optimizer state but fewer parameters
question_bank.explanation: Checkpointing saves memory by dropping some forward activations and recomputing them later, which increases compute but reduces stored state.
